title: Mexican Scallop Gratin
description: A Mexican-inspired twist on the classic French scallop gratin. This dish features succulent scallops topped with a mixture of intensely flavorful spices, creamy cheese, and crunchy French breadcrumbs.
instructions: Rinse the scallops and pat them dry with paper towels.
Season the scallops with salt, black pepper, cumin, chili powder, and paprika.
In a large skillet, heat a tablespoon of oil over medium-high heat.
Add the scallops and sear for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until browned.
Transfer the scallops to a baking dish.
In the same skillet, add the crushed French bread and cook for 1-2 minutes, or until lightly toasted.
Sprinkle the toasted bread over the scallops.
Drizzle the Mexican crema over the bread.
Sprinkle the grated cheese over the top.
Bake for 10-12 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Sprinkle the intensely flavored chili flakes over the top.
